name: org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin
description:
GW Maven Plugin (Ghostwriter Maven Plugin) is a Maven plugin that integrates MachAI Ghostwriter guided-workflow (GW) document processing into Java/Maven builds. It scans a documentation/source tree (often src/site, or a user-specified directory) for embedded @guidance blocks and processes/rewrites content using a configured GenAI provider/model. It supports both aggregator-style execution (including running without a pom.xml) and standard reactor-style execution for multi-module builds.

Primary goals (Mojos):
- gw:gw (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GW): Aggregator goal (requiresProject=false) that can run in directories without a pom.xml. Uses session-based module discovery and runs processing similar to the Ghostwriter CLI. Supports Maven parallel session settings by forwarding degreeOfConcurrency to the processor. Handles workflow termination failures by converting ProcessTerminationException into MojoExecutionException.
- gw:reactor (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ReactorGW): Runs in standard Maven reactor build context. Can delay processing of the execution-root project until other reactor projects have completed using gw.rootProjectLast (default true), particularly useful for parent POM projects. Uses a daemon thread to defer execution-root scanning when applicable.
- gw:act (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.Act): Interactive aggregator goal that applies a predefined "action" prompt across scanned documents using ActProcessor. The action prompt comes from -Dgw.act or is collected interactively via Maven Prompter. Supports -Dgw.acts to point to a directory of predefined action definitions.
- gw:act-reactor (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ReactorAct): Reactor-friendly variant of gw:act intended to run in the execution-root project context; overrides module processing to a no-op and scans only the target project.
- gw:clean (org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.Clean): Deletes temporary artifacts created by GW processing under the module base directory.

Shared configuration and behavior (AbstractGWGoal):
- gw.model: Provider/model identifier used by the workflow (e.g., openai:gpt-4o-mini). Read via Ghostwriter.GW_MODEL_PROP_NAME and/or PropertiesConfigurator.
- gw.scanDir: Optional scan root override. If not provided, defaults to the Maven execution root directory (AbstractGWGoal sets it from session.getExecutionRootDirectory()).
- gw.instructions: Instruction locations (file paths or classpath locations) consumed by the workflow.
- gw.guidance: Default guidance text forwarded to the workflow.
- gw.excludes: Exclude patterns/paths to skip while scanning.
- gw.logInputs (default false): Whether to log the list of input files passed to the workflow.
- gw.genai.serverId: Reads credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> and forwards them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD via PropertiesConfigurator.

Implementation notes:
- Built on org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter and the processors GuidanceProcessor and ActProcessor.
- Detects Maven project layout using MachAI ProjectLayout/MavenProjectLayout integration to resolve effective models in multi-module sessions.
- Supports non-recursive invocation detection (e.g., parent with modules vs. only one project in session) and forwards that to processors.

Typical use cases include documentation automation, guided file processing with embedded @guidance blocks, interactive document actions, CI/CD documentation maintenance, and integrating GenAI-backed transformations into Maven build lifecycles.

usageContext: Aggregator goal execution without a pom.xml
Guided file processing with embedded @guidance blocks

features.name: gw:gw
features.description: Aggregator Maven goal (requiresProject=false) that runs Ghostwriter guided workflow document processing. It configures a GuidanceProcessor with shared parameters inherited from AbstractGWGoal, supports non-recursive invocation detection, forwards Maven parallel degreeOfConcurrency when session is parallel, defaults gw.scanDir to the execution root directory when not provided, and executes scanning/processing. Converts workflow ProcessTerminationException into MojoExecutionException.

examples.description: Run guided workflow processing in the current directory.
Run in a directory without a pom.xml (goal supports requiresProject=false).
Select a provider/model, override scan root, and enable input-file logging.
Load GenAI credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml and forward them as GENAI_USERNAME/GENAI_PASSWORD.

features.name: gw:reactor
features.description: Reactor goal that processes documents in a standard Maven reactor build context. Supports gw.rootProjectLast (default true) to defer execution-root project processing (especially for pom packaging) until other reactor projects complete. Uses shared configuration and scan execution from AbstractGWGoal and converts ProcessTerminationException to MojoExecutionException.

examples.description: Run guided workflow processing in reactor context.
Defer processing of the execution-root project until other reactor projects finish (default behavior for pom packaging).
Override the scan root directory.
Load GenAI credentials from Maven settings.xml and forward them to the workflow.

features.name: gw:act
features.description: Interactive aggregator goal that applies an "action" prompt over scanned documents using ActProcessor. The action prompt is provided via -Dgw.act or collected interactively via Maven Prompter (multi-line supported via Ghostwriter.MULTIPLE_LINES_BREAKER). Supports -Dgw.acts to point to a directory of predefined action definitions. Inherits scanning, excludes, model, credential forwarding (gw.genai.serverId), and logging parameters from AbstractGWGoal and forwards Maven parallel degreeOfConcurrency when session is parallel.

examples.description: Run interactive action mode and prompt for action text.
Run action mode with an explicit action prompt and scan root override.
Use a directory of predefined actions and enable input-file logging.

features.name: gw:act-reactor
features.description: Reactor-friendly variant of gw:act that targets the execution-root project in a standard reactor build. It configures an ActProcessor for the execution root and overrides module processing as a no-op. It marks execution as non-recursive and then delegates to the shared Act processing flow (including optional interactive prompt collection).

examples.description: Run action processing for the execution-root project using reactor context.
Run with an explicit action prompt and scan root override.

features.name: gw:clean
features.description: Deletes temporary artifacts created by MachAI Ghostwriter guided workflow processing under the current module base directory by calling GuidanceProcessor.deleteTempFiles(basedir). Typically bound to Maven's clean phase.

examples.description: Delete workflow temporary files for the current module.
Run Maven clean and then delete GW workflow temporary files.

features.name: Shared scan execution and settings.xml credential forwarding
features.description: Common scan/execute flow used by goals to configure a GuidanceProcessor/ActProcessor (excludes, instructions, default guidance, scan root selection, optional input-file logging) and run document scanning. Determines project basedir, defaults scanDir to session execution root when not set, and logs usage via GenAIProviderManager. When gw.genai.serverId is provided, reads username/password from Maven settings.xml <servers><server> and forwards them as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD via PropertiesConfigurator.

examples.description: Run processing with scan root override, instructions, default guidance, excludes, and input logging enabled.
Load GenAI credentials from settings.xml and forward them to the workflow.

customizations.name: Maven settings-based GenAI credentials forwarding
customizations.description: Provide gw.genai.serverId to load credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> and forward them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD properties via PropertiesConfigurator.

examples.description: Reads username/password from the specified settings.xml server entry and forwards them to the workflow.

customizations.name: Scan root override
customizations.description: Use gw.scanDir to override the scan root directory. When omitted, the plugin defaults to the Maven execution root directory (and uses the project basedir as the project root for scan resolution).

examples.description: Scan documentation under src/site.

customizations.name: Scan filtering with excludes
customizations.description: Use gw.excludes to configure exclude patterns/paths that should be skipped when scanning documentation sources.

examples.description: Exclude build output and VCS directories from scanning.

customizations.name: Deferred execution-root processing in reactor mode
customizations.description: Use gw.rootProjectLast (default true) to delay processing of the execution-root project until other reactor projects complete, typically useful when the execution-root project uses pom packaging.

examples.description: Defers execution-root processing until other modules are done.

customizations.name: Interactive action prompt selection
customizations.description: Use gw.act to supply an action prompt non-interactively; if not set, the plugin prompts via Maven Prompter. Use gw.acts to point to a directory of predefined action definitions.

examples.description: Supply the action prompt via a system property (non-interactive).
Use a directory of predefined action definitions.

customizations.name: Parallel session concurrency forwarding
customizations.description: When Maven is running in parallel mode (session.isParallel()), the plugin forwards the session request degreeOfConcurrency to the underlying processors (GuidanceProcessor/ActProcessor) via setDegreeOfConcurrency.

examples.description: Run Maven in parallel mode and allow the plugin to forward degreeOfConcurrency to its processors.

examples.title: Basic guided workflow processing
examples.description: Runs guided workflow processing in the current directory.

examples.title: Run without a pom.xml
examples.description: Uses the aggregator goal that does not require a Maven project.

examples.title: Reactor processing with scan root override
examples.description: Processes documentation under src/site in reactor context.

examples.title: Interactive action mode
examples.description: Prompts for an action prompt and applies it to scanned documents.

examples.title: Use Maven settings credentials
examples.description: Loads credentials from settings.xml and selects a provider/model identifier for the workflow.
